headline: Khazanah Reports RM12B Paper Loss on Global Tech Bets
context: Sovereign wealth fund Khazanah Nasional disclosed a RM12 billion unrealised loss in its global technology portfolio for 2025, primarily from holdings in Chinese tech firms amid US-China tensions.

cards.big: Khazanah lost RM12 billion on paper from tech bets — equal to 4% of annual national budget
cards.sub: Alibaba, JD.com, and ByteDance-linked investments all declined 25-40% in 2025

cards.big: Khazanah's net worth fell to RM85 billion from RM98 billion — first decline since 2018 reset
cards.sub: Fund's 5-year annualised return of 3.1% underperforms EPF's 5.4% over same period

cards.big: RM12 billion loss could have funded 48,000 affordable homes at RM250,000 each for B40 families
cards.sub: Opportunity cost of sovereign wealth misallocation directly impacts social spending capacity

cards.big: Khazanah's investment committee of 9 members includes no independent external fund management expert
cards.sub: Board composition lacks the institutional investor representation standard in Singapore's Temasek model

cards.big: When sovereign wealth is gambled on foreign tech hype, it's ordinary Malaysians who absorb the loss
Should Khazanah be required to maintain 60% domestic allocation with parliamentary oversight on foreign bets?
